gazette: Official Gazette dated 28 April 2023 and numbered 32174
title: Communiqué on the Export of Ozone Depleting Substances and Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases (Export: 2023/4)
bodyHtml:
The Regulation on the Export of Ozone Depleting Substances and Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases (Export: 2023/4) has been published.

In accordance with the Beijing Amendment to the Montreal Protocol, the export of goods listed in Annex-1 and Annex-2 with their HS codes and descriptions cannot be made to countries that are not parties to the Beijing Amendment (not included in Annex-A).

The export of hydrofluorocarbons and mixtures containing hydrofluorocarbons listed in Annex 4 of the regulation is subject to the approval of the Ministry of Environment, Urbanization, and Climate Change.

For exports of chemicals listed in Annex-4 in bulk as pure, used, recycled, or reclaimed hydrofluorocarbons after May 10, 2023, a hydrofluorocarbon control certificate will be required for each shipment.

Individuals or legal entities who wish to export chemicals listed in Annex-4 in bulk in containers must register with the Ministry of Environment, Urbanization, and Climate Change's FARAVET program to obtain a control certificate.

The hydrofluorocarbon export control certificate closing process will be carried out by the exporting company through the FARAVET application in accordance with the usage guide, no later than 15 calendar days after the shipment has taken place. In case the shipment does not take place, the exporter will also close the certificate through FARAVET.

In addition, the export of goods listed in Annex-3/A with their HS codes and descriptions, which include substances specified in the table in Article 5 of the regulation, and goods listed in Annex-3/B with their HS codes and descriptions that operate with any of the goods specified in the table are also prohibited.

To view the Regulation on the Export of Ozone Depleting Substances and Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases (Export: 2023/4), please click here.
